reports.title: Null GEX values at session start (Tick 1)
reports.summary: At the start of the simulation (tick 1, 9:30 AM), GEX values for both SPY and SPX are observed as null.
reports.suggested_action: No action required; continue monitoring data ingestion and calculation as the session progresses past the opening tick.

reports.summary: Strong negative SPY movement is occurring, but GEX (Gamma Exposure) values for both SPY and SPX remain null at 10:10 AM (tick 41).
reports.suggested_action: Investigate data source or calculation engine for GEX, as values remain null 40 minutes into the session despite active market data and trading.
reports.title: Persistent Null GEX Values in Active Session

evidence: recentJournal[0]: TRANSMISSION_STATE TRANSMISSION_DELAYED SPX:0.31 SPY:0.03 failedTicks:1
reports.suggested_action: Immediate engineering review of GEX data ingestion and transmission stability. Data remains null over an hour into the session, and transmission is now flagged as delayed with failed ticks.
reports.summary: GEX values remain null (data defect), and transmission status has degraded to TRANSMISSION_DELAYED.
reports.title: Persistent GEX Nulls and Transmission Delay
Trader Journal refers to GEX walls despite null data
evidence: recentJournal[1]: AI_TICK_REFLECTION NONE: High-frequency intent flipping on flat price. Textbook PIN behavior invalidating trend thesis. Standing aside. GEX walls adjusted slightly up.
reports.suggested_action: Critical: Repair the GEX data feed immediately. Verify if the trader journal entries referring to "GEX walls adjusted" are hallucinated by the model due to missing real data, or if valid wall data exists but is not being propagated to the 'gexSpy/gexSpx' fields in the snapshot context.
reports.summary: Market data 'gexSpy' and 'gexSpx' fields remain null, yet the trader's journal entry at 11:26 AM refers to "GEX walls adjusted slightly up," indicating a critical disconnect between displayed data and assumed trader state.

reports.title: Midday GEX Nulls and Unreliable Transmission
reports.summary: Persistent critical data failure: GEX values are still null, and transmission status is RELATIONSHIP_UNRELIABLE near midday.
reports.suggested_action: Critical: fix GEX data unavailability and unstable transmission. 'gexSpy' and 'gexSpx' have been null since session start (tick 1), representing a total failure of this data stream over 160 minutes. Transmission quality remains poor.

Investigate price fill logic and option pricing model. A CALL position exited via MAX_LOSS_LIMIT when the underlying spot price moved *up* (from 748.04 at entry to 748.11 at exit) indicates either extreme IV crush, a wide bid/ask spread, or a calculation bug.
reports.summary: A CALL option position was stopped out for a -31.6% loss after one minute via MAX_LOSS_LIMIT, despite the underlying spot price increasing slightly from 748.04 to 748.11.
evidence: t: 12:10 PM, action: CANONICAL FILL BUY CALL 751C @$0.19
reports.title: CALL Max Loss Exit on Favorable Spot Movement

reports.suggested_action: Review the 12:10 PM Call entry logic, specifically the 'vehicleFail 38% catastrophic 50%' parameters and the entry spot relation to regime, to determine if the thesis was immediately invalid or if execution timing was suboptimal.

reports.title: Repeated Rapid Loss on Entry
evidence: Recent Trades: t:2:30 PM action: CANONICAL FILL BUY PUT 747P @$0.20 entrySpot: 748.7 exitSpot: 748.6613073092291
Recent Trades: t:2:31 PM action: THESIS-EXIT 747P @$0.14 MAX_LOSS_LIMIT result: -30.0% ($-490) pnl: -29.999999999999993
reports.summary: Second rapid drawdown and MAX_LOSS_LIMIT exit immediately after Put entry.
reports.suggested_action: Urgently review context and setup logic for the 2:30 PM Put trade. This is the second instance of an immediate, substantial loss triggered by the MAX_LOSS_LIMIT within 1 minute of entry, indicating a critical failure in entry timing, directional validity, or risk validation upon fill.

Conduct a comprehensive review of entry logic. This is the third separate instance in this session of an immediate directional failure leading to a MAX_LOSS_LIMIT exit within 1 minute of contract fill. The journal entry at 3:27 PM confirms the violent adverse reversal, suggesting critical mistiming or misreading of structural regime, especially following a similar loss at 2:30 PM.

reports.suggested_action: Perform a comprehensive failure analysis on all four instances of immediate MAX_LOSS_LIMIT exits following Canonical Fills in this session (12:11 PM, 2:31 PM, 3:21 PM, 3:32 PM). The 3:32 PM exit resulted in a -57.9% PnL within one minute, indicating the invalidation logic and trade setup parameters (vehicleFail, catastrophic) are completely failing to manage risk at entry.

Acknowledge the post-session state transition and the activation of the 'DRAWDOWN_REVIEW_REQUIRES_MODEL_CHANGE' blocker, indicating the system successfully identified excessive drawdown and locked out further trading per protocol.
reports.summary: System correctly locked trading intent post-close based on defined drawdown limits.
